title: compat_settings dereference without null check in multiple functions
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/adapter.c

Several functions call compat_settings() or compat_settings_const() and check for NULL before use, but the pattern is inconsistent.

Good pattern (with check):
- Line 185: if (settings != NULL && settings->advert_name_valid)
- Line 231: if (settings != NULL && settings->device_pin_valid)
- Line 399-400: if (settings != NULL && settings->channel0_valid)

Some store_* functions check for NULL and return error:
- Line 661-663: if (settings == NULL) return error

This is defensive and correct. However, a code reviewer seeing both patterns might assume NULL is impossible and remove checks in the future.

Consider: standardize on one approach - either always check and handle NULL, or use _Nonnull annotation on compat_settings and never check.
close_reason: Standardized on _Nonnull for compat_settings in adapter_ops (header + helpers), removed impossible NULL checks in all 6 modifier functions (reset, store_*). Query paths retain defensive valid-flag checks. All derefs now safe per annotation. Verified no other call sites. 3 review passes found no further issues; tests pass in native_sim context.
